documents.title: OpenSSL Heartbleed
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: OpenSSL
Severity: Critical
Summary: The Heartbleed bug allows memory disclosure from affected OpenSSL implementations.
Affected area: TLS heartbeat processing.
Defensive guidance: patch OpenSSL, rotate private keys, revoke and reissue certificates, and invalidate affected sessions.

documents.title: GNU Bash Shellshock
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: GNU Bash
Severity: Critical
Summary: Environment variables can trigger arbitrary command execution in vulnerable Bash versions.
Affected area: command parsing.
Defensive guidance: patch Bash, review CGI exposure, and inspect logs for suspicious header-based command injection.

documents.title: SMBv1 EternalBlue
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Microsoft Windows SMBv1
Severity: Critical
Summary: Remote code execution through specially crafted SMB packets.
Affected area: SMBv1 service.
Defensive guidance: disable SMBv1, apply Microsoft patches, isolate exposed hosts, and hunt for lateral movement.

documents.title: Apache Struts Jakarta Multipart Parser RCE
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Apache Struts 2
Severity: Critical
Summary: Crafted Content-Type headers can lead to remote code execution.
Affected area: Jakarta multipart parser.
Defensive guidance: patch Struts immediately, inspect inbound request logs, and monitor for web shell behavior.

documents.title: Citrix ADC Directory Traversal
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Citrix ADC / Gateway
Severity: Critical
Summary: Path traversal can expose or enable execution through the management stack.
Affected area: VPN and appliance gateway paths.
Defensive guidance: patch appliances, remove exposed management interfaces, and review logs for unusual traversal patterns.

documents.title: Zerologon
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Microsoft Netlogon
Severity: Critical
Summary: Weak cryptographic validation in Netlogon allows domain compromise.
Affected area: domain controller authentication.
Defensive guidance: patch domain controllers, enforce secure RPC usage, and review DC events for suspicious machine account changes.

documents.title: Microsoft Exchange ProxyLogon
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Microsoft Exchange Server
Severity: Critical
Summary: Server-side request forgery can lead to authentication bypass and further compromise.
Affected area: Exchange web services.
Defensive guidance: patch Exchange, investigate web shell indicators, rotate credentials, and preserve IIS logs.

documents.title: PrintNightmare
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Windows Print Spooler
Severity: Critical
Summary: Vulnerable spooler behavior can allow remote code execution or privilege escalation.
Affected area: print spooler service.
Defensive guidance: patch Windows, disable spooler on systems that do not need it, and monitor driver installation events.

documents.title: Log4Shell
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Apache Log4j
Severity: Critical
Summary: JNDI lookup processing can lead to remote code execution in vulnerable Log4j versions.
Affected area: application logging.
Defensive guidance: upgrade Log4j, block risky outbound connections, search logs for JNDI patterns, and rotate secrets if exposure is confirmed.

documents.title: Spring4Shell
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Spring Framework
Severity: Critical
Summary: Data binding in certain deployments can allow remote code execution.
Affected area: Java web application binding.
Defensive guidance: patch Spring, review deployment patterns, and check for unexpected file writes or web shell artifacts.

documents.title: Microsoft Outlook NTLM Leak
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Microsoft Outlook
Severity: Critical
Summary: Specially crafted reminders can trigger NTLM credential leakage.
Affected area: Outlook calendar processing.
Defensive guidance: patch Outlook, review malicious reminder artifacts, and hunt for suspicious SMB or outbound authentication attempts.

documents.title: MOVEit Transfer SQL Injection
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Progress MOVEit Transfer
Severity: Critical
Summary: SQL injection can lead to data theft and server compromise.
Affected area: MOVEit web application.
Defensive guidance: patch immediately, inspect for web shell indicators, rotate credentials, and review transferred data exposure.

documents.title: Citrix ADC RCE
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Citrix ADC / Gateway
Severity: Critical
Summary: Memory corruption can lead to unauthenticated remote code execution.
Affected area: appliance gateway services.
Defensive guidance: patch exposed appliances, monitor for suspicious child processes, and validate appliance integrity.

documents.title: Citrix Bleed
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: NetScaler ADC / Gateway
Severity: Critical
Summary: Session token leakage can expose authenticated sessions.
Affected area: VPN and gateway session handling.
Defensive guidance: patch the appliance, invalidate sessions, rotate credentials, and review remote access logs.

documents.title: Atlassian Confluence Authorization Bypass
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Atlassian Confluence
Severity: Critical
Summary: Authorization bypass can allow attacker-controlled administrative actions.
Affected area: Confluence setup and admin workflows.
Defensive guidance: patch Confluence, inspect audit logs, and review unauthorized account or configuration changes.

documents.title: Fortinet FortiOS SSL VPN RCE
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Fortinet FortiOS
Severity: Critical
Summary: Out-of-bounds write in SSL VPN can allow remote code execution.
Affected area: VPN service.
Defensive guidance: patch appliances, monitor VPN logs, and check for suspicious administrative or process activity.

documents.title: XZ Utils Backdoor
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: XZ Utils
Severity: Critical
Summary: Backdoored releases can undermine SSH authentication and system trust.
Affected area: library supply chain.
Defensive guidance: remove affected builds, verify package provenance, and revalidate system integrity.

documents.title: PAN-OS GlobalProtect Command Injection
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: Palo Alto PAN-OS
Severity: Critical
Summary: Command injection in GlobalProtect can allow unauthenticated execution.
Affected area: firewall edge services.
Defensive guidance: patch immediately, inspect device telemetry, and review lateral movement indicators from exposed gateways.

documents.title: PHP CGI Argument Injection
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: PHP CGI
Severity: Critical
Summary: Improper argument handling can lead to remote code execution on some deployments.
Affected area: CGI mode execution.
Defensive guidance: patch PHP, avoid CGI mode where possible, and inspect web logs for crafted query-string execution attempts.

documents.title: OpenSSH regreSSHion
documents.content:
Vendor/Product: OpenSSH
Severity: High
Summary: A signal handler race can allow remote code execution in some server configurations.
Affected area: sshd authentication handling.
Defensive guidance: patch OpenSSH, reduce exposure of internet-facing SSH, and monitor authentication and crash telemetry.
